message:
test(fcp-conformance/hrw): pin select_coordinator + rank_nodes_by_hrw determinism + consistency (br-vxtqw)

select_coordinator and rank_nodes_by_hrw are the deterministic HRW
(rendezvous hashing) primitives that let every mesh peer
independently arrive at the SAME coordinator for a given (zone,
subject) WITHOUT cross-peer communication. They back LeaseCoordinator
(br-9nee8) and AuthorityView (br-rybvy) but had no direct conformance
coverage.

Adds 10 tests against the public fcp_core::{select_coordinator,
rank_nodes_by_hrw} API:

- empty_eligible_set_yields_none_coordinator
- single_node_eligible_set_returns_that_node
- select_coordinator_is_deterministic_under_repeated_calls
- select_coordinator_is_invariant_under_input_reordering (THE
  property that prevents distributed disagreement when peers
  receive discovery snapshots in different orders)
- rank_nodes_by_hrw_first_element_equals_select_coordinator (API
  consistency contract — callers can interchange them)
- rank_nodes_by_hrw_is_a_total_permutation_of_eligible_set (no
  drops, no extras — failover routing depends on the full list)
- rank_nodes_by_hrw_is_deterministic_under_repeated_calls
- rank_nodes_by_hrw_is_invariant_under_input_reordering
- different_subjects_distribute_coordinators_across_nodes
  (statistical: 200 subjects across 5 nodes, every node must win
  at least once — failure probability < 4e-20 if HRW is uniform)
- different_zones_can_yield_different_coordinators_for_same_subject
  (zone genuinely contributes to the hash — otherwise zone-private
  mappings could be replayed across zones)

All 10 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
hrw_coordinator_selection_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
